title: Using consistent file and folder naming
what_it_is: A naming convention is a fixed pattern you use every time you name a file or folder — for example always starting with the date, then the topic, then the file type.
why_use_it: When every file follows the same pattern, a learner can tell what something is and when it's from without opening it, and files sort into a sensible order automatically.
how_to: Decide on a pattern before you start adding files — for example: YYYY-MM-DD_Topic_Type, so a file might be named 2026-07-23_Unit1_Worksheet.
Use the same pattern for folders too — for example Unit1_Resources rather than a mix of different styles across folders.

Avoid spaces at the start of a name, and avoid special characters like / \ : * ? " < > | — these can cause errors when a file is opened on some devices. Use an underscore or hyphen instead of a space if you prefer.
When you rename an existing file, right-click it in OneDrive, Teams or File Explorer, select Rename, type the new name in your chosen pattern, then press Enter.
Once a pattern is agreed for a shared space, keep it consistent — a mismatched name is often the reason a learner can't find something they know exists.
